title: Angular Guard Options Schema
description: Creates a new route guard in your project. Route guards are used to control access to parts of your application by checking certain conditions before a route is activated. This schematic generates a new guard with the specified name, type, and options.

name.description: The name for the new route guard. This will be used to create the guard's class and spec files (e.g., `my-guard.guard.ts` and `my-guard.guard.spec.ts`).
name.x-prompt: What name would you like to use for the guard?

skipTests.description: Skip the generation of a unit test file `spec.ts` for the new guard.

flat.description: Creates the new guard files at the top level of the current project. If set to false, a new folder with the guard's name will be created to contain the files.

path.description: The path where the guard files should be created, relative to the current workspace. If not provided, the guard will be created in the current directory.

project.description: The name of the project where the guard should be created. If not specified, the CLI will determine the project from the current directory.

functional.description: Generate the guard as a function instead of a class. Functional guards can be simpler for basic scenarios.

implements.description: Specifies the type(s) of guard to create. You can choose one or more of the following: `CanActivate` (controls access to a route), `CanActivateChild` (controls access to child routes), `CanDeactivate` (asks for confirmation before leaving a route), `CanMatch` (determines if a route can be matched).
implements.x-prompt: Which type of guard would you like to create?

typeSeparator.description: The separator character to use before the type within the generated file's name. For example, if you set the option to `.`, the file will be named `example.guard.ts`.
